source_docs.title: inDrop Nature Protocols Supplementary Text and Figures

oligos.name: Acrydite-modified barcoding primer (hydrogel bead capture oligo)
oligos.role: Acrydite/photocleavable DNA primer covalently incorporated into the hydrogel bead; carries the T7 promoter and the partial PE1/Read handle onto which the two split-and-pool barcodes are later extended

components.name: 5' Acrydite + photocleavable (PC) spacer
components.role: Covalent attachment to hydrogel mesh + UV-photocleavable release (1-(2-nitrophenyl)-ethyl)

components.name: T7 RNA polymerase promoter
components.role: constant; drives in-vitro transcription during linear amplification

components.name: Partial TruSeq Read 1 / PE1 handle
components.role: PE1 / Read 2 & index-read primer annealing site

oligos.notes: Ordered HPLC-purified from TriLink/IDT. Light-sensitive; used at 50 µM in the acrylamide-primer bead mix. Barcode1 (with the added CCGATCT), the W1 spacer, barcode2, UMI and poly-dT are added onto the 3' -CGCTCTTC end by two rounds of split-and-pool primer extension.

oligos.name: Fully assembled barcoding RT primer (photoreleased from bead)
oligos.role: Photoreleased reverse-transcription primer; captures mRNA via poly(dT) and tags each transcript with a two-part cellular barcode + UMI (Fig. 6c)

components.name: T7 promoter + PC spacer (upstream, on bead)
Cell barcode 1
components.role: first split-and-pool cellular barcode (one of 384; exact sequences in Supplementary Table 3, not included; length 8-11 nt in inDrops)

components.name: W1 spacer
components.role: constant spacer between the two barcodes

components.name: Cell barcode 2
components.role: second split-and-pool cellular barcode (one of 384; Supplementary Table 4)

oligos.notes: 147,456 unique barcodes = 384 x 384 (two split-and-pool rounds). PE1, W1 and T19V are shared by all full-length primers and are the FISH-QC targets. Barcode building-block sequences are provided in Supplementary Tables 3 and 4 (not included here).

oligos.name: Barcode 1 plate oligonucleotides (split-and-pool round 1)
oligos.role: Barcoded DNA oligonucleotides annealed and Bst-extended onto bead primers in the first split-and-pool round
oligos.notes: 384 sequences across four 96-well 'Barcode 1 plates'; exact sequences listed in Supplementary Table 3 (not included in the provided documents).

oligos.name: Barcode 2 plate oligonucleotides (split-and-pool round 2)
oligos.role: Barcoded DNA oligonucleotides annealed and Bst-extended onto bead primers in the second split-and-pool round
oligos.notes: 384 sequences across four 96-well 'Barcode 2 plates'; exact sequences listed in Supplementary Table 4 (not included in the provided documents).

oligos.name: BA19 oligo (T19V hybridization/blocking oligo)
oligos.role: Poly(dA) oligo hybridized to the bead poly(dT) tails to protect full-length primers during ExoI digestion of primers lacking a poly-T tail
oligos.notes: 5' B (not-A) anchor + 19 A. Used at 1 mM during enzymatic cleanup (Steps 71-76).

oligos.name: FAM-PE1 QC probe
oligos.role: Fluorescent FISH probe targeting the PE1 handle for bead-synthesis QC
oligos.notes: 5' 6-FAM label. Reverse complement of the CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT handle.

oligos.name: FAM-W1 QC probe
oligos.role: Fluorescent FISH probe targeting the W1 spacer for bead-synthesis QC (confirms first barcode extension)

oligos.name: FAM-BA19 QC probe (T19V)
oligos.role: Fluorescent FISH probe targeting the poly(dT) tail for bead-synthesis QC (confirms full-length primer)

oligos.name: PE2-N6 primer (random-hexamer RT primer)
oligos.role: Random-hexamer reverse-transcription primer used on fragmented amplified RNA; introduces the partial PE2/Read 1 handle

components.name: Partial PE2 / Read 1 handle
components.role: becomes the Read 1 primer binding site

components.name: Random hexamer
components.role: primes fragmented aRNA at random positions

oligos.name: PE1 library PCR primer (P7 + i7 index)
oligos.role: Library-amplification primer that adds the Illumina P7 capture sequence and the i7 library (sample) index at the barcode end

components.name: i7 sample index
components.role: library multiplexing index (XXXXXX; 24 variants in Supplementary Table 2; indices 6 and 12 required when multiplexing)

oligos.notes: Standard desalting. Mixed 1:1 with PE2 primer for library PCR/qPCR.

oligos.name: PE2 library PCR primer (P5)
oligos.role: Library-amplification primer that adds the Illumina P5 capture sequence at the transcript end

components.name: Partial PE2 / Read 1 handle
components.role: anneals to the PE2-N6 introduced handle

oligos.name: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom Illumina Read 1 primer; anneals at the PE2/Read 1 handle and reads into the transcript

oligos.name: Custom index-read sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom index (i7) read primer; anneals at the read2/index handle on the barcode side and reads the library index
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified; 100 µM. Do not mix with standard Illumina index primers.

oligos.name: Custom Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Custom Illumina Read 2 primer; anneals at the PE1/barcode handle and reads the cellular barcode + UMI
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified; 100 µM. Read 2 = cell barcode + UMI read (>=50 bp).

final_library.source_label: inDrops v2 (Zilionis et al., Nat. Protoc. 2017) — final Illumina-compatible library after PE1/PE2 PCR
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC GGTCTC GGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT [CDNA] AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA [UMI:6] [CELL_BARCODE:8] AAGGCGTCACAAGCAATCACTC [CELL_BARCODE:8] AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG [SAMPLE_INDEX:6] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: GGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT = Read 1 primer site (partial PE2 / TruSeq Read 1 handle)
AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG = Read 2 / index-read primer site (partial TruSeq Read 1, revcomp of PE1 handle)

evidence.locator: inDrops v2 (Zilionis et al., Nat. Protoc. 2017) — final Illumina-compatible library after PE1/PE2 PCR

library_generation.title: mRNA capture & in-drop reverse transcription (barcoding)
library_generation.summary: Photoreleased bead RT primer captures cellular mRNA on its poly(dT) tail and SuperScript III reverse-transcribes it, tagging cDNA with the cell barcode + UMI.
library_generation.note: After UV (365 nm) photocleavage, the barcoding primer is free in-drop. Its poly(dT) anneals to mRNA poly(A) and reverse transcription (SuperScript III, 50 °C) synthesizes first-strand cDNA carrying the T7 promoter, PE1 handle, barcode1, W1, barcode2, UMI.

library_generation.title: cDNA:mRNA hybrid after RT
library_generation.summary: First-strand cDNA:mRNA hybrid with a single-stranded T7-promoter/PE1 handle.
library_generation.note: The target is a cDNA-mRNA hybrid; the T7 promoter and PE1 handle remain single-stranded, which makes them immune to the dsDNA-specific HinfI cleanup in the next step.

library_generation.title: Enzymatic cleanup (ExoI + HinfI)
library_generation.summary: ExoI digests unused ssDNA primers and HinfI cleaves the T7 promoter within primer dimers/hairpins, leaving the barcoded hybrid intact.
library_generation.note: 3'->5' exonuclease ExoI removes leftover single-stranded primers; FastDigest HinfI cleaves the double-stranded T7-promoter sequence present in primer dimers. The barcoded cDNA:mRNA hybrid is immune to both (ssDNA T7 promoter).

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: NEBNext second-strand synthesis converts the barcoded hybrid into double-stranded DNA carrying a double-stranded T7 promoter.
library_generation.note: Generates dsDNA that provides a functional double-stranded T7 promoter for in-vitro transcription.

library_generation.title: Linear amplification by IVT (T7)
library_generation.summary: HiScribe T7 RNA polymerase transcribes from the T7 promoter to linearly amplify the barcoded library as antisense RNA (aRNA).
library_generation.note: CEL-seq-style linear amplification; each cDNA molecule yields many antisense RNA copies, preserving the barcode/UMI end.

library_generation.title: Fragmentation of amplified RNA
library_generation.summary: Zinc-catalyzed hydrolysis fragments the aRNA to sequenceable size.
library_generation.note: RNA Fragmentation Reagents (70 °C, ~3 min); only fragments retaining the barcode/UMI (T7-proximal) end will ultimately be amplifiable.
library_generation.product:
aRNA fragments (~200-400 nt), including the barcode/UMI-bearing 3' fragment:
5'- ...[UMI:6-rc][CELL_BARCODE:8]UUCCGCAGUGUUCGUUAGUGA[CELL_BARCODE:8]AGAGCGUCGUGUAGGGAAAGAG... -3'

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription with random hexamer (PE2-N6)
library_generation.summary: PrimeScript RT with the PE2-N6 random-hexamer primer converts fragmented aRNA back to cDNA, introducing the PE2/Read 1 handle.
library_generation.note: The PE2-N6 primer anneals at random positions along each aRNA fragment and appends the TCGGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT handle.

library_generation.title: Library amplification PCR (PE1 + PE2)
library_generation.summary: Kapa HiFi PCR with PE1 and PE2 adds P5, P7 and the i7 library index, producing the final Illumina-ready library.
library_generation.note: PE2 (P5) primes at the PE2-N6 handle (transcript end); PE1 (P7 + i7 index) primes at the PE1/barcode handle. qPCR determines cycle number (typically 8-12). Final library averages 400-600 bp.

library_sequencing.note: Transcript / cDNA read. The primer is identical to the top-strand Read 1 handle, so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads the cDNA insert. Read length is platform-dependent: 89 cyc (MiSeq), 37-43 cyc (NextSeq), 61 cyc (HiSeq 2500).
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...GAGATCTACACGGTCTCGGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT[CDNA]... -3'  (top)
3'- ...CTCTAGATGTGCCAGAGCCGTAAGGACGACTTGGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[CDNA]... -5'  (bottom = template)
                     5'-GGCATTCCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCT------->
                        Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand and reads the cDNA transcript

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6-nt i7 library (sample) index. The primer is identical to the top-strand handle, so it anneals to the bottom strand and extends rightward into the index. Omitted (0 cycles) when a single library is run without multiplexing.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'  (top)
3'- ...TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATCCCTTTCTCNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'  (bottom = template)
    5'-AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG----->
                                     index-read primer reads NNNNNN = i7 index off the bottom strand

library_sequencing.note: Reads cell barcode 1, the W1 spacer, cell barcode 2 and the UMI (>=50 bp required). The primer is the reverse complement of the top-strand handle, so it anneals to the top strand and reads leftward: BC1 -> W1 -> BC2 -> UMI.
library_sequencing.diagram:
5'- ...[UMI:6][CELL_BARCODE:8]AAGGCGTCACAAGCAATCACTC[CELL_BARCODE:8]AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAG... -3'  (top = template)
3'- ...[UMI:6][CELL_BARCODE:8]TTCCGCAGTGTTCGTTAGTGAG[CELL_BARCODE:8]TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATCCCTTTCTC... -5'  (bottom)
                                                            <-------CTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT-5'
       Read 2 primer anneals to the top-strand handle and reads BC1 - W1 - BC2 - UMI leftward

title: inDrops (v2)
description: inDrops is a droplet-microfluidic single-cell RNA-seq method that co-encapsulates single cells with barcoding hydrogel beads (BHBs) bearing photoreleasable, combinatorially barcoded RT primers. After UV release of primers, mRNA is captured on a poly(dT) tail and reverse-transcribed in-drop so that each cell's transcripts are tagged with a two-part cellular barcode plus a UMI. Pooled cDNA is linearly amplified by T7 in-vitro transcription (CEL-seq strategy), the amplified RNA is fragmented and re-reverse-transcribed with a random-hexamer primer, and a limited-cycle PCR adds Illumina P5/P7 and an i7 library index. Read 1 reads the transcript, Read 2 reads the cell barcode + UMI, and the index read reads the library (sample) index.

reference.label: inDrop Nature Protocols Supplementary Text and Figures

original_publication.title: Single-cell barcoding and sequencing using droplet microfluidics

throughput.summary: >15,000 barcoded cells per hour (~12,000+ cells/h; ~5 cells/s); one BHB synthesis batch barcodes 500,000-1,000,000 cells. Typically 3,000-30,000 cells per run, processed as libraries of 1,000-3,000 cells.
throughput.cells: Captures ~60-90% (typically ~75%) of input cells; requires >=2,000 input cells (>10,000 preferred)
throughput.rna: ~2,350-14,508 UMI-filtered molecules per cell depending on sequencing depth (~100-200 K reads/cell)
throughput.dna: Final library average size 400-600 bp; concentration typically 3-20 nM

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (cell arrival into droplets modeled as a Poisson process; UMI-based absolute transcript counting corrects PCR amplification bias)

other.label: Primers per bead
other.value: ~10^9 photoreleasable DNA primers per hydrogel bead

other.label: Amplification strategy
other.value: CEL-seq-style linear amplification by T7 in-vitro transcription

other.label: Commercial variants
other.value: 1CellBio (licensed inDrops); 10x Genomics offers a related droplet method

data_processing.summary: Raw reads are processed by the inDrops custom low-level pipeline (distributed as Supplementary Software) into a genes-by-cells, UMI-filtered count matrix. Cell-associated barcodes are called by manually thresholding a reads-per-barcode abundance histogram; retained reads are filtered for barcode abundance and read quality, transcript reads are aligned to a reference transcriptome, and molecules are collapsed by UMI (a unique cell-barcode x gene x UMI combination = one UMI-filtered molecule). Downstream high-level clustering/population-structure analysis is platform-independent and not part of this pipeline.
